faqs.question: What is programmatic SEO?
faqs.answer: Programmatic SEO creates large numbers of pages automatically from structured data—product catalogs, location databases, comparison matrices, etc. Instead of writing each page manually, you build templates and data pipelines that generate unique, optimized pages at scale. Examples include Zapier's app integration pages, Yelp's location pages, and NerdWallet's comparison pages.

faqs.question: Won't Google penalize auto-generated content?
faqs.answer: Google penalizes thin, low-quality auto-generated content. Programmatic SEO done right creates genuinely useful pages with unique data, proper structure, and real value for users. The key is combining templates with rich data sources and quality controls—not spinning the same generic content across thousands of pages.

faqs.question: What data do I need for programmatic SEO?
faqs.answer: You need structured data with enough unique attributes to create differentiated pages. Common sources include: product catalogs with specifications, location databases, comparison matrices, user-generated data, API integrations, and industry datasets. If you have data that answers specific search queries, programmatic SEO can work.

faqs.question: How many pages can programmatic SEO create?
faqs.answer: Technically unlimited, but the right number depends on your data quality and search demand. Some projects generate hundreds of pages; others create tens of thousands. I help you identify the sweet spot where each page targets real search volume and provides genuine value—quality matters more than quantity.
